text:
Seiko Hui Guo Rou (セイコ＝ホイコーロ, Seiko Hoikōro) is the Sixth Queen of the Kakin Empire's eight Queens and sixth wife of Nasubi Hui Guo Rou. She gave him a pair of twin daughters, 10 th Prince Kacho Hui Guo Rou and 11 th Prince Fugetsu Hui Guo Rou. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 

Appearance

Seiko is a woman with short, fair hair that curls sideways. She has eyebags under her clear eyes, giving her the appearance of being middle-aged. She is most commonly seen wearing a light-colored, long-sleeved dress. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

Personality

Unlike some of the other Queens, Seiko seems to care about having one of her daughters win the succession contest, though whether this is for her or for her daughters is unclear. She seems to favor her younger daughter Fugetsu over her older daughter Kacho. She often insists on having her way when no one of importance to her is present, but treats her daughters with love.

Seiko seems to be distrustful and uncooperative in general, refusing to answer any question during her initial interview with Kaiser when he was first sent to escort her in Room 1010 after she was accused of planning to assassinate Woble, despite being told that nothing said during their interview would be formally recorded. This insistence in getting her way is seen again when she fights to prevent Kacho's room from being cordoned off after Kacho drops out for as long as possible, even stationing guards outside to physically prevent soldiers from entering. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 

Tears well up in Seiko's eyes when the relatively bratty Kacho comforts her and declares her intent to fight in the succession contest with dignity and pride. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 377 It is unclear if her care and concern for Kacho is genuine, considering her perception of Kacho being a shield for Fugetsu. 

When it comes to the more meek Fugetsu, Seiko seems slightly more genuine, comforting and hugging her when she is escorted back from Tier 3. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 381 Again, the genuineness of Seiko's care and concern for Fugetsu is unclear, considering her thoughts of being able to get Fugetsu to do as she wants. 

Perhaps part of Seiko's true personality is seen through her reaction to Melody's performance, enthusiastically volunteering to arrange for her Sunday banquet performance on the eighth day of the voyage to be broadcast throughout the Black Whale 1. 

During a rant by Kacho to cover her secret communication, Kacho says that Seiko's tearing up was an empty gesture to assuage her guilt that made her sick, and calls her useless. However, given that Kacho was putting up a front, it is unclear how much of what she says is reflective of her true opinion of her mother. Kaiser's comment that Seiko meddles with Fugetsu is an indication of his perception of Seiko's relationship with Fugetsu, that Melody and "Kacho" do not disagree with. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 402 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

6 th Queen Seiko is first seen as a silhouette introducing the eight wives of Kakin's King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou and later introduced as the mother of 10 th Prince Kacho and 11 th Prince Fugetsu when Kakin's 14 Princes are first revealed. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 Later, during the eve of departure festival, Seiko and the rest of Kakin's royal family are flown to Tier 1 of the Black Whale 1, where they reside for the duration of the voyage. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 

As the Princes begin to leave from the departure ceremony on the first day of the voyage, one of 14 th Prince Woble's guards (working for 1 st Queen Unma) explains the hierarchy of Queens. It is shown that Fugetsu has two guards chosen by Seiko and five guards acting as spies chosen by the five higher queens, one guard-spy per queen. It is also shown that 12 th Prince Momoze has one guard chosen by her mother, 7 th Queen Sevanti, and six guards acting as spies chosen by the six higher queens, one guard-spy per queen, including one chosen by Seiko, and that Woble, daughter of 8 th Queen Oito, had seven guards acting as spies chosen by the seven higher queens, including one now deceased guard-spy from Seiko. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 

On the first day of the voyage, Sevanti orders most of Momoze's guards and servants to serve 13 th Prince Marayam instead of her, and leaves Momoze with six guards, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 361 including Seiko's guard-spy Nagmum, who lies in bed waiting for an opportunity to assassinate her. That same day, Seiko's guards Ryoji and Bachaem are seen worrying about guarding Fugetsu from the other guards. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

Later that day, both Kacho and Fugetsu are contacted by Room 1014, offering them both the chance to send two of their men to learn Nen under Kurapika. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 Loberry is one of the servants that Kacho sends, and turns up at 9 a.m. the next day, the second day of the voyage, at Room 1014. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 Shortly after the participants introduce themselves, Loberry notices a mysterious woman with a weird mask. She starts to panic and causes a distraction, and Barrigen, Marayam's guard, is killed in the resulting commotion. Sakata and Hashito, guards of 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei, arrest her and contact Supreme Magistrate Cleapatro to request Seiko be charged under suspicion of being an accomplice to Barrigen's murder and plotting to assassinate Woble. Cleapatro denies Sakata's request for not clearing the Royal Family privilege protection regulation. Instead of having Seiko arrested, Cleapatro dispatches an investigator to monitor Room 1010, Kacho's residence, for 72 hours. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 

Shortly after, the investigator turns up at Room 1010 with two other men and tries to conduct an interview with Seiko. Despite his reminder that she is protected under royal privilege and that their procedure is just a formality that would not enter court records, Seiko insists on remaining silent and refuses to answer any questions. The investigator accepts this and ends the interview, then lets her know that they would have to live with each other for 72 hours, but Seiko is free to move about with supervision, and that almost any request made by her would be honored. 

On the third day of the voyage, Kacho suggests performing at the coming Sunday banquet on the eighth day of the voyage, saying that they should do something as the hosts, that she wanted to play the glass-harp with "Foo-foo" as a duet, and that her guard Melody can play the flute. Seiko is moved and smiles, and tells Kacho that she thinks it is a good idea, and agrees to make another appeal to Nasubi. As Seiko leaves, Kacho reassures her mother that she would fight with dignity and pride and would not be afraid, making tears well up in Seiko's eyes. As soon as Seiko leaves, Kacho disparages her to Kaiser, to cover the sound of her device secretly communicating the progress of her escape plan to Melody. 

On the fourth day of the voyage, Seiko receives Fugetsu back in Room 1011 after being escorted back from Tier 3. As Seiko hugs her, the investigator tells Fugetsu that she would be assigned an escort for 72 hours but not be arrested, and was free to go to the banquet on Sunday. Seiko returns with the investigator to Room 1010, where they listen to Melody perform an interpretation of Hyle's Requiem. Seiko listens intently and is enthralled, and enthusiastically applauds Melody after. Seiko asks if they would hear the piece again on Sunday, and Melody tells her that she would prepare something more appropriate instead, so she enthusiastically promises to be there and promises Melody to arrange for the ship-wide broadcast of her performance. 

On the ninth day of the voyage, Kurapika awakens Kacho's other participating representative Yuri to Nen. Taking advantage of their temporary seclusion from the other participants, Kurapika explains his intention to prevent further victims from the succession battle to Yuri, and Yuri agrees to his request to tell Kacho and Seiko, with a positive spin, that they are planning more classes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 388 

On the tenth day of the voyage, two guards guard the entrance of Room 1010 from the four soldiers present to cordon off Room 1010 in line with the regulations for Princes that "drop out" of the succession contest. On the other side of the door, Seiko angrily argues over the phone and insists on being allowed to gather Kacho's personal effects before regulations are enacted. Seiko also demands to know why she could not see Fugetsu, currently held in the Justice Bureau on Tier 2 after her unsuccessful escape attempt with Kacho, despite being her mother and a Queen. Seiko expresses her worry about Fugetsu and once again demands to see her, saying that cordoning off the room is unacceptable until she gets to see Fugetsu. She also insists on talking to Fugetsu by herself when the time is right and that the officials must go through her, then slams the phone back onto the receiver. 

Seiko thinks of how Fugetsu can best continue participating, and decides that she would rather let the others think that Kacho, who died during her escape attempt with Fugetsu, is alive as long as possible. Seiko thinks about the misfortune of Kacho "dropping out" so early on, since despite hating her know-it-all attitude, Kacho was an important shield for Fugetsu. As she directs Kacho's former employees in packing up the room, Seiko decides that it is safest for Fugetsu to stay protected at the Justice Bureau for as long as possible, since even though she could get Fugetsu to do her bidding, Fugetsu is too weak on her own. 

On the eleventh day of the voyage, Kaiser, the investigator, describes his motivations for helping "Kacho", Fugetsu, and Melody to "Kacho" and Melody, and says that he thinks it would be best for Fugetsu to take the throne and change the whole system, and that having Kacho by her side would protect her from Seiko's meddling.

Manga Appearances

Trivia

The names of Kakin's eight Queens loosely reflect their order. "Sei" means "six" in Italian, and variants of "Sei" mean "six" in other languages used across Europe.
